# Bit System AI Package

Bit System is the internal design system for 2P Growth Lab.
It is used across:
- landing pages
- institutional site pages
- commercial proposals
- monthly reports
- pitch decks
- social media materials

This AI package exists because raw HTML and CSS are not a reliable universal interface for every model.
The files in ./ai provide:
- a compact reading order
- machine-readable contracts
- explicit source precedence
- usage rules by context

## Read this package in order

1. ./index.md
2. ./system.md
3. ./system.json
4. ./decision-rules.json
5. ./components.json
6. ./recipes.json
7. ./templates.json
8. ./assets.json
9. ../tokens.json
10. ../assets/manifest.json

## What this system is

Bit System is not only a component library.
It is a combined brand system and operational design system with:
- dark and light themes
- design tokens
- CSS runtime
- layout primitives
- UI components
- brand recipes
- asset governance
- documentation for humans and AI

## What to trust first

Trust order:
1. Files in ./ai
2. ../tokens.json
3. ../assets/manifest.json
4. ../tokens.css and ../components.css
5. ../SKILL.md and ../docs/ai-guide.md
6. ../pages and ../examples as visual reference only

## Core brand rules

- Visual tone: retro-tech, high contrast, gamer accents, clean base.
- Display type: VT323 only.
- Body and UI type: Inconsolata only.
- Primary brand color: neon #00FFD1.
- Accent color: fuchsia #FF00FF.
- Deep structural color: tolopea #2409A9.
- Default theme: dark.
- Light theme is mainly for proposals, reports, and editorial contexts.

## Core interaction rules

- Use semantic tokens in new implementation work.
- Do not rely on primitive colors directly in new components.
- Use one primary neon CTA per viewport at most.
- Prefer recipes before custom composition.
- Prefer current assets over legacy ones.

## Runtime implementation

Public CSS entrypoints:
- ../tokens.css
- ../components.css

Canonical structural files:
- ../tokens.json
- ../assets/manifest.json
- ../assets/icons.svg

## Important limitation

The CSS runtime is not yet generated automatically from ../tokens.json.
When tokens and runtime disagree, treat ../tokens.json as the structural contract and verify runtime behavior in the CSS modules.
